text.title: api-testing Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for shaping a REST/JSON API test suite as a pyramid with OpenAPI-validated bodies and oasdiff-gated CI.

rule.statement: Maintain a pyramid: unit tests >= 70% of suite count, integration tests <= 25%, contract (Pact) tests only at cross-team / cross-service boundaries, end-to-end tests <= 5% and only for revenue-critical paths. Fail CI when the shape inverts beyond these ratios.

rationale.source: Mike Cohn — Succeeding with Agile, 2009; updated practice 2020-2025

rule.rationale: Inverted pyramids (ice-cream cone) take 10-100x longer to run, are flaky, and obscure root cause. The shape gate forces engineers to push assertions down the stack.

rule.statement: Every integration test that produces a response MUST validate the body against the OpenAPI schema for the called operation (status, headers, content-type, body shape). Status-code-only assertions are forbidden in integration tests.
rule.rationale: Status-code-only assertions miss the most common contract regressions (renamed fields, dropped properties, changed types). Schema validation catches these for free; the author writes one `validate_against_schema(response, op_id)` instead of N field-level asserts.

rule.statement: Generate response fixtures FROM the OpenAPI schema (Schemathesis, hypothesis-jsonschema). Do not hand-craft response shapes and let them drift from the spec.
rule.rationale: Schema-derived fixtures stay in sync with the spec automatically; hand-crafted ones rot the moment the spec evolves.

rule.statement: Pact (or equivalent CDC) contract tests fire only at cross-team or cross-service boundaries. Inside a single team's monorepo, prefer in-process integration tests against the schema. Pact has a real maintenance cost and only pays back at organizational seams.
rule.rationale: Pact's value is decoupling deploy cadence between teams. Inside one team, schema-validated integration tests deliver the same regression catch at a tenth of the operational overhead.

rule.statement: Each PR snapshots `openapi.json` (or `.yaml`) and runs `oasdiff` against the base branch. Breaking changes block the merge unless the PR carries the `breaking-change-approved` label. Non-breaking changes are listed in the PR description automatically.
rule.rationale: oasdiff classifies changes (path removed, response type narrowed, required field added, etc.) deterministically. A semantic diff gate catches accidents that semver bumps alone don't.

rule.statement: Run Schemathesis (or equivalent property-based fuzzer driven from the OpenAPI spec) nightly OR on every PR touching `views/`. The fuzzer must assert (a) no 5xx for any spec-compliant input and (b) every response validates against its schema.
rule.rationale: Property-based fuzzing finds edge cases hand-written tests miss (boundary values, encoding edge cases, header combinations). Two cheap assertions ("no 5xx" + "response matches schema") cover the most common API regressions.
